records.ety: [OE. arai, arrai, OF. arrai, arrei, arroi, order, arrangement, dress, F. arroi; a (L. ad) + OF. rai, rei, roi, order, arrangement, fr. G. or Scand.; cf. Goth. raidjan, garaidjan, to arrange, MHG. gereiten, Icel. reiði rigging, harness; akin to E. ready. Cf. Ready, Greith, Curry.]

defs: Order; a regular and imposing arrangement; disposition in regular lines; hence, order of battle; as, drawn up in battle array.
quotes: Wedged together in the closest array.

defs: The whole body of persons thus placed in order; an orderly collection; hence, a body of soldiers.
quotes: A gallant array of nobles and cavaliers.
Their long array of sapphire and of gold.

defs: Dress; garments disposed in order upon the person; rich or beautiful apparel.
A ranking or setting forth in order, by the proper officer, of a jury as impaneled in a cause.
The whole body of jurors summoned to attend the court.

records.ety: [OE. araien, arraien, fr. OE. arraier, arreier, arreer, arroier, fr. arrai. See Array, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Arrayed (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Arraying.]

defs: To place or dispose in order, as troops for battle; to marshal.
quotes: By torch and trumpet fast arrayed, Each horseman drew his battle blade.
These doubts will be arrayed before their minds.

defs: To deck or dress; to adorn with dress; to cloth to envelop; -- applied esp. to dress of a splendid kind.
quotes: Pharaoh . . . arrayed him in vestures of fine linen.
In gelid caves with horrid gloom arrayed.

defs: To set in order, as a jury, for the trial of a cause; that is, to call them man by man.
